subject: Biology
title: Antigens: The Enemy's ID
description: Learn Antigens: The Enemy's ID in one minute.

hook.text: Every pathogen carries unique molecular shapes on its surface—antigens. Your immune system reads them.

buildup.text: Antigens are proteins or sugars that T cells and B cells recognize as foreign.

discovery.text: Your immune system ignores your own antigens—a process called self-tolerance learned in development.

twist.text: Blood type is determined by antigens on red blood cells. Wrong transfusion? Your immune system attacks.

climax.text: Antigen recognition is so precise that one amino acid change can make a pathogen invisible to immunity.

punchline.text: The immune system reads IDs. Fakes get destroyed.

quiz.question: What determines your blood type?
options: Antigens on the surface of red blood cells
The number of white blood cells
